name: jsdom
usage_summary: Not imported directly in any source file — no `import 'jsdom'` or `require('jsdom')` exists anywhere under packages/. It is used implicitly as the Vitest test environment: both packages/ui-extensions-dev-console/vite.config.mts and packages/ui-extensions-server-kit/vite.config.mts set `test.environment: 'jsdom'`, which causes Vitest to load jsdom to simulate a browser DOM for React component testing.
how_to_remove: Switch the vitest `environment` from `'jsdom'` to `'happy-dom'` in both vite.config.mts files and replace `"jsdom": "^25.0.0"` with `"happy-dom"` in both package.json devDependencies. happy-dom is a lighter-weight DOM implementation that is the standard Vitest alternative. Some test helpers relying on jsdom-specific behaviour (e.g. exact error messages) may need minor adjustment.
replacement_reason: jsdom is only used as a Vitest test environment for DOM simulation in two React packages. happy-dom is a faster, maintained alternative that Vitest officially recommends and supports natively. The switch is mechanical (one config key per package) with minimal risk of test breakage.
